title: Making pi-context a globally installable command, grounded in pi's own bin/publish pattern
question: Grounded in the canonical pi runtime as exemplar, how is @davidorex/pi-context-cli shipped so the `pi-context` command (including the spec'd `pi-context --pi-bound` mode) resolves on PATH from any directory, not only via in-repo `node packages/pi-context-cli/dist/bin.js`?
method: Read-only code inspection of the installed pi runtime (resolved via `which pi`) and packages/pi-context-cli + packages/pi-project-workflows + packages/pi-context, cross-checked against the pi-bound spec (R-0004); npm publish state verified via `npm view @davidorex/<pkg> version`.
scope: npm registry state of all six @davidorex/* packages
findings_summary:
Pi's global-command recipe (the exemplar): `type:module` + `bin:{"pi":"dist/cli.js"}`, a `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang on the BUILT entry, an explicit `chmod +x dist/cli.js` inside the build script (so npm can symlink the command to an executable on global install), `files:["dist",…]` shipping only built output, and all runtime deps declared and shrinkwrapped so they nest under the package's own node_modules and resolve regardless of cwd.

pi-context-cli already matches pi on type/bin/shebang/files/publishConfig. The gaps: (1) its build never sets the exec bit on dist/bin.js (it is -rw-r--r--), which blocks `npm link` and weakens `npm i -g` — FGAP-047; (2) for --pi-bound it must declare @davidorex/pi-project-workflows so require.resolve resolves the meta-package from an installed CLI, which the published 0.30.0 does not — FGAP-048.

Publish state (verified via npm view): all six @davidorex/* are published at 0.30.0 (pi-context, pi-context-cli, pi-project-workflows, pi-behavior-monitors, pi-jit-agents, pi-agent-dispatch). So the base `pi-context` command is registry-installable today (`npm i -g @davidorex/pi-context-cli` pulls the published dep tree); an earlier draft's claim that none was published conflated 'not installed on this machine' (checking /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules) with 'unpublished' and is wrong.

Concrete paths: (a) local dogfooding — add the build chmod, then `npm link` from packages/pi-context-cli puts pi-context on PATH with deps resolving through the existing workspace symlinks; (b) registry — already available for the base command; --pi-bound additionally requires declaring the meta-dep and cutting a new (release-gated) version. Recommended publish unit: CLI-as-own-unit (pi-faithful, lowest delta), recorded as DEC-0013; the meta-package has no dist/bin and folding the CLI there would be a larger restructure.

--pi-bound plugs in with no extra wiring once pi-context is global (it is a CLI process mode at the bin's main(), DEC-0014). The one global-install-specific risk is whether require.resolve('@davidorex/pi-project-workflows/package.json') and the downstream `pi install -l <metaRoot>` resolve correctly from a REGISTRY-installed CLI — npm link only simulates the workspace-symlink path, so this needs a published-package smoke test (TASK-029/030 orchestrator criteria, release-gated).

dependencies: @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent — its package.json bin/build/files/shrinkwrap is the global-install exemplar

stale_conditions: pi-context-cli's build adds chmod +x dist/bin.js — FGAP-047 closes and the exec-bit gap framing is superseded.
A new @davidorex/* release supersedes 0.30.0 — the published-version facts must be re-verified.
pi (@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent) changes its bin/build/files/shrinkwrap pattern — the exemplar recipe shifts.
scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh or the pi-bound spec changes the meta-path resolution or tool-union derivation.

citations.label: pi build sets the exec bit (the step pi-context-cli lacks)
